title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: root service capability declarations or service-level models depending upward on sealed capability modules, any lifecycle service implementation or package entrypoint depending on its outward bindings, provider code reaching current-main through governance module or binding imports instead of the neutral root dependency, CLI access to the retired governance binding, module router bypass of module context, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, package or CLI-local provider-binding value imports outside the native-resource binding and provider-resource composition root, release-binding values outside the exact transitional export reader or mutation-capable release-binding values even there, pure release values outside command parsing, caller-owned releases/packaging/providers artifact or evidence projections, a second production value consumer of the lifecycle service client, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: Root capability declarations remain independent of sealed governance, packaging, providers, releases, and vendors implementations, and that sealed set grows monotonically as context-direction slices land. Governance owns current-main selection while one closed service-composition middleware derives its neutral observation reader from the raw content-workspace capability. The CLI selects one raw artifact repository provider and explicit root for lifecycle service context; lifecycle middleware privately derives artifact and evidence semantics from that pair for releases, packaging, and providers. The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and derives provider-domain repositories under module middleware. The root composes module routers through impl.router. Runtime construction narrows through one exact CLI composition root. Provider-transition values enter only at the native-resource binding and provider composition root. The transitional public release binding remains explicit deletion debt, while its sole admitted CLI value import is the reader-only export composition root and cannot flow back into service context. Pure release algebra enters command parsing through the release surface.
remediate: Declare root capabilities and cross-module observation contracts under service/model, derive current-main once in service/middleware/current-main.ts from the raw content-workspace capability and governance-owned selector, select the raw artifact repository once at the CLI composition root, derive lifecycle artifact and evidence semantics in service/middleware/artifacts.ts through private service repositories, consume those capabilities through provided context, derive provider repositories and adapters under module provided context, project exact capabilities into each module from module.ts, implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep remaining ports protocol/type-only, construct the lifecycle client only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/client.ts, use type-only lifecycle and provider-binding dependencies elsewhere, import package provider-transition values only at the native-resource binding and provider composition root, keep the release-binding transition limited to the read-only artifact reader at the exact export composition root, and import pure release parsers directly from the release surface in commands/input.ts.
message: Lifecycle dependencies flow from root-owned capabilities and neutral cross-module observations into sealed module contexts and handlers, then through impl.router and exact CLI composition roots; pure release algebra does not flow through host bindings.
